name: P1 foundation loop smoke/e2e acceptance spec

scenarios.title: Login then bootstrap exposes publish capability inside 我的 Skill and keeps admin menus gated
coverage: P1 navigation keeps publish inside 我的 Skill while review/admin stay permission-gated
preconditions: Seed user exists
API is reachable
Desktop starts from clean local state
steps: Log in with the seed P1 user.
Fetch desktop bootstrap context.
Open the desktop shell navigation.
expectedEvidence: Login succeeds and stores an authenticated session.
Bootstrap returns user, connection, counts, features and navigation.
Navigation contains home, market, my_installed, tools, projects, notifications, settings only.
publishSkill is true for authenticated users, while reviewWorkbench and adminManage remain false for normal users.

scenarios.title: Market search, filter and sort compose correctly
preconditions: Seed market data includes multiple departments, compatible tools and risk levels
steps: Run a keyword search with compatibleTool and riskLevel filters.
Change sort from composite to latest_published.
Verify pagination metadata.
expectedEvidence: Returned items match the requested filters.
Sort order changes deterministically.
Pagination includes page, pageSize, total and hasMore.

scenarios.title: Restricted detail does not leak private package data
preconditions: Seed includes one summary-only Skill and one inaccessible Skill
steps: Open a summary-only Skill detail.
Open a no-access Skill detail or direct URL.
expectedEvidence: Summary-only response excludes README, audit/review summary, packageURL, packageRef and packageHash.
No-access response uses stable permission_denied or skill_not_found error code instead of text-only denial.

scenarios.title: Install succeeds after SHA-256 verification and writes Central Store state
preconditions: Installable seed Skill has packageHash=sha256:<hex>
Central Store is empty
steps: Request a download-ticket for install.
Download the package and compute SHA-256.
Install the package.
expectedEvidence: download-ticket includes packageURL, packageHash, packageSize, packageFileCount and expiresAt.
Computed package hash matches the ticket.
Central Store receives the package atomically after hash validation.
local_skill_installs records localHash and sourcePackageHash.

scenarios.title: Hash mismatch blocks install and preserves old state
preconditions: A corrupt/tampered package fixture is available
steps: Request a ticket whose expected hash does not match the tampered package.
Attempt install.
Inspect Central Store and local DB state.
expectedEvidence: Install fails with hash_mismatch and retryable=true.
Temporary package files are deleted.
Central Store and existing local_skill_installs records remain unchanged.

scenarios.title: Update warns before overwriting locally changed package
preconditions: Installed Skill localHash differs from sourcePackageHash
A newer installable version exists
steps: Open the update action for the modified local Skill.
Confirm that overwrite warning is shown.
Cancel once, then confirm on a second attempt.
expectedEvidence: UI warns that local content changed and update will overwrite.
Cancel leaves local state unchanged.
Confirmed update revalidates hash and replaces Central Store atomically.

scenarios.title: Enable to Codex succeeds by symlink without copying source content
preconditions: Codex target path is writable and supports symlinks
Skill is installed in Central Store
steps: Enable the installed Skill to Codex with default mode.
Inspect target path and local enabled target record.
Inspect queued local event payload.
expectedEvidence: Target path is a symlink to the adapter artifact output.
enabled_targets records requestedMode=symlink, resolvedMode=symlink and fallbackReason=null.
local event payload includes requestedMode and resolvedMode.

scenarios.title: Symlink failure falls back to copy and records fallbackReason
preconditions: Target path is configured to reject symlink creation
Skill is installed
steps: Enable with requestedMode=symlink into the restricted target.
Inspect target path and enabled target record.
Inspect local event payload.
expectedEvidence: Enable succeeds via copied artifact.
enabled_targets records requestedMode=symlink, resolvedMode=copy and a structured fallbackReason.
local event payload includes fallbackReason.

scenarios.title: Disable removes only managed target and preserves Central Store
preconditions: Skill is installed and enabled to at least one target
steps: Disable one enabled target.
Inspect target path and Central Store package path.
Inspect local notification/event state.
expectedEvidence: Managed target symlink or copy is removed.
Central Store package remains intact.
A disable_result notification/event is recorded.

scenarios.title: Uninstall lists and removes Central Store plus managed targets after confirmation
preconditions: Skill is installed and enabled to multiple managed targets
steps: Start uninstall.
Verify all managed target paths are listed for confirmation.
Confirm uninstall.
Inspect Central Store and target directories.
expectedEvidence: Confirmation lists all managed enabled targets.
Confirmed uninstall removes Central Store package and managed targets only.
Partial failures are reported with failed paths if any target cannot be removed.

scenarios.title: Offline enable/disable queues events and survives app restart
preconditions: Skill is installed locally
API is unreachable or app is forced offline
steps: Enter offline mode.
Enable then disable the installed Skill.
Restart the desktop app.
Inspect local state and queue.
expectedEvidence: Market search/install/update are disabled while local installed actions remain available.
offline_event_queue persists enable/disable events with stable event IDs.
Restart preserves local installed state, target state and queued events.

scenarios.title: Restored connection syncs local-events idempotently without governance mutation
preconditions: offline_event_queue contains duplicate/retryable events
API is reachable again
steps: Flush local events after network recovery.
Repeat the flush with the same event IDs.
Inspect server response and local queue status.
expectedEvidence: First flush returns acceptedEventIDs for unsynced events.
Duplicate flush returns ignoredDuplicateEventIDs or equivalent idempotent result.
Server does not mutate unrelated permission, review, publish, delist or version governance state.
Local queue marks synced events without losing failed/retryable events.

scenarios.title: Browser publish flow submits ZIP/folder and drives admin review actions
preconditions: API, PostgreSQL, Redis and MinIO are running
Desktop Vite app is connected to the real API
steps: Log in as a normal author and submit one folder package and one ZIP package.
Wait for system precheck to move each submission into 待审核 or 待人工复核.
Log in as an eligible admin, claim a review, handle人工复核 if required, then approve it.
Return to the market and confirm the published Skill becomes visible.
expectedEvidence: Publisher list shows workflowState, latestSubmissionID and withdraw availability.
Review detail shows precheck results, package download link and available action buttons.
Approved submissions write the new version into market-facing skills data.
Permission changes keep the original visibility until approval and switch only after approval.

scenarios.title: Browser return-for-changes flow supports folder resubmission and later publish
preconditions: API, PostgreSQL, Redis and MinIO are running
Desktop Vite app is connected to the real API
steps: Log in as a normal author and submit one folder package.
Log in as an eligible admin and return the review for changes.
Resubmit the same skillID from the browser with updated folder contents.
Approve the resubmitted review.
expectedEvidence: Returned submissions expose returned_for_changes state and keep market hidden.
Resubmission creates a new latest submission for the same skillID.
Approved resubmission writes the skill into the market-facing dataset.

scenarios.title: Rejected publish attempts never leak into market visibility or download-ticket paths
preconditions: A new publish submission exists and is reviewable
steps: Log in as an eligible admin and reject the new publish review.
Search the market for that skillID.
Attempt to fetch detail or download-ticket through the normal user path.
expectedEvidence: Rejected submissions move into review_rejected with history retained.
The rejected skill is absent from market search results.
Normal-user detail/download paths do not expose installable package data for the rejected skill.

scenarios.title: Permission change keeps old market visibility until approval, then switches atomically
preconditions: A published public_installable skill exists
At least one outsider user can observe market access changes
steps: Create a permission_change submission that narrows visibility/access.
Check market access for an outsider user before approval.
Approve the permission change review.
Check the outsider market view again.
expectedEvidence: Before approval, outsider users still see the old visibility/install state.
After approval, outsider users lose installability and only see the new summary/detail contract.
The switch happens only after review approval, not at submit time.

scenarios.title: Update publish keeps the old market version live until review approval
preconditions: A published v1 skill exists and a v2 package is available for submission
steps: Submit an update review for v2.
Before approval, open the market detail and inspect the visible version.
Approve the update review.
Open the market detail and download-ticket again.
expectedEvidence: While v2 is pending, market still exposes v1 as the current version.
After approval, market switches to v2.
download-ticket resolves against the newly approved version only after approval.

scenarios.title: The same newly published skill can be downloaded, installed, enabled, restarted, and uninstalled through Electron desktop local runtime
preconditions: A newly published skill from the happy-path browser flow exists
Electron desktop local-state harness can access the real API
steps: Use the exact same skillID/version published in the browser happy path.
Fetch a real download-ticket for that skill.
Install through the Electron desktop bridge, enable to one tool and one project, restart local state, then uninstall.
expectedEvidence: download-ticket hash matches the downloaded ZIP archive.
Local install records sourcePackageHash as the ZIP hash and localHash as the installed content hash.
Restart restores enabled targets from SQLite.
Disable/uninstall remove managed targets and Central Store state for that same published skill.

scenarios.title: Notifications support mark-read and offline cache display
preconditions: Seed user has unread notifications
Desktop has performed one online notification sync
steps: Open notification center and mark one notification as read.
Mark all as read.
Switch offline and reopen notifications.
expectedEvidence: Unread count updates after single and all mark-read actions.
local_notifications cache shows recent notifications offline.
Offline state clearly indicates data is cached.

scenarios.title: Electron preload exposes only approved desktop bridge methods and rejects unsafe renderer access
preconditions: Electron main/preload sources are present.
steps: Inspect the preload API exposed to the renderer.
Verify the renderer cannot access raw ipcRenderer, ipcMain, filesystem, or child process APIs.
Exercise unknown channel and unsafe external URL rejection paths in the static/runtime smoke.
expectedEvidence: contextIsolation is enabled and nodeIntegration is disabled.
Only typed desktopBridge methods are exposed.
Unknown channels and unsafe external URL protocols are rejected.

scenarios.title: Electron first launch preserves existing local Store data or reports a recoverable conflict
preconditions: A fixture legacy local Store source exists or migration tests create one.
steps: Launch migration with no source and verify fresh initialization.
Launch migration with existing skills.db and central-store into an empty Electron userData path.
Re-run migration and verify idempotent success without deleting source data.
Run conflict and partial-copy failure cases.
expectedEvidence: skills.db and central-store are copied or safely referenced in Electron userData.
Migration manifest records source, target, version, and outcome.
Conflicts and partial failures keep source data intact and surface recovery instructions.

scenarios.title: Electron updater preserves P1 manual ticket, download, verify, confirm, launch, and event flow
preconditions: A client update release artifact is available from a local or fixture update server.
steps: Request a release download-ticket.
Download the installer artifact and reject size or hash mismatches.
Verify signature status using the platform path, preserving skipped_non_windows where appropriate.
Require explicit user confirmation before launching the installer.
Report download, hash, signature, and installer launch events.
expectedEvidence: hash_failed and signature_failed events are reported for failed verification.
installer_started is reported only after ready-to-install and explicit confirmation.
Event reporting failures do not break an otherwise successful update.

scenarios.title: Release gates reject active legacy desktop runtime references in product delivery paths
preconditions: All Electron migration lanes have been integrated.
steps: Run the legacy runtime reference scan over package manifests, desktop source, tests, scripts, verification config, and active docs.
Allow only explicit migration-history artifacts that are not part of runtime delivery.
expectedEvidence: No active runtime, script, verification, or docs path refers to the removed desktop runtime.
The scan fails if a removed runtime global, source directory, package script, or dependency reappears.
